steps.explanation: Find the mean of each dataset. All five sets sum to 235 (e.g., A: 44+45+47+49+50=235), so every dataset has mean \(\bar{x} = 235 \div 5 = 47\). Standard deviation depends only on the deviations from the mean.

The variance is (sum of squared deviations) ÷ 5. Set A: 5.2, Set B: 3.2, Set C: 2.0, Set D: 0.4. Set D has the smallest variance and therefore the smallest standard deviation (\(\sqrt{0.4} \approx 0.63\)).

• Choice A: {44,45,47,49,50} — deviations up to ±3; sum of squares = 26; SD ≈ 2.28. Largest spread. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: {45,45,47,49,49} — deviations up to ±2; sum of squares = 16; SD ≈ 1.79. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: {45,46,47,48,49} — deviations up to ±2; sum of squares = 10; SD ≈ 1.41. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: {46,47,47,47,48} — deviations only ±1 with three values at the mean; sum of squares = 2; SD ≈ 0.63. Smallest standard deviation. That matches choice D — D is correct.
